response.justification: Bogdan is a capable full-stack and distributed systems engineer with a broad portfolio, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's hard requirements. The job mandates 8 years of C++ experience as a minimum qualifier; Bogdan's resume lists C++ only in a bachelor thesis project and a brief stint at National Instruments (roughly 6 months), and C++ does not even appear in his skills section. Without this core technical foundation, he cannot credibly lead a C++-based cloud compute platform. Additionally, he has no demonstrated formal technical leadership over cloud or infrastructure engineering teams, and his infrastructure exposure, while present, is incidental rather than the primary focus of his career.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed micro-services (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby), showing relevant systems-level thinking
Consistent application of engineering best practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple roles, aligning with the role's expectation to establish coding standards
Hands-on DevOps and infrastructure exposure (Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, GitLab CI) indicating operational awareness beyond pure application development
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (one short role at NI, one thesis project) against an 8-year hard minimum — the single most critical disqualifier for this role
No evidence of formal technical leadership over a team of engineers; mentoring activity appears informal (code katas, workshops) rather than organizational ownership
Cloud platform work is shallow and project-scoped (GISCollective on Kubernetes, AWS CDK at Optilyz) rather than production-grade platform engineering at scale
